summary: Matched several Tournament mode gmtou functions and tightened related game-mode declarations. The PR replaced multiple gmtou stubs with C implementations for on-enter/on-leave archive setup and cleanup, animation timer callbacks, and JObj animation-frame selection logic. It also introduced Tournament-specific data structures such as TmData, TmBoxArrays, and TmAnimTimers, converted gm_804771C4 from an untyped array placeholder to TmData, and improved prototypes in gmtou.h and gm_17C0.h from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS/UNK_T to concrete HSD_GObj, HSD_JObj, void, char*, and TmData* types. Review feedback highlighted an important header hygiene issue: .static.h headers containing static/data definitions should only be included from .c files, not public headers, because including them elsewhere can perturb data/bss sections.
what_changed: Implemented gm_8019B8C4_OnEnter in gmtou.c, including Tournament archive loads for GmTou1p, TmBox.dat, GmTou3p, and GmTou4p, SIS_TournamentData setup through HSD_SisLib_803A62A0, and random selection of audio IDs 0x5D or 0x5E.
Implemented gm_8019B9C8_OnLeave and gm_8019EE54_OnLeave to release HSD_Archive pointers through lbArchive_80016EFC.
Implemented several gmtou animation/update callbacks using HSD_GObj and HSD_JObj, including fn_8019BF18, fn_8019BF8C, fn_8019C6AC, fn_8019CBFC, and fn_8019CFA4.
Added a new gmtou.static.h containing Tournament-local structs TmBoxArrays and TmAnimTimers, plus an extern for gm_804771C4.
Added struct TmData to src/melee/gm/types.h with early fields x0 and x4, padding to 0x4E0, and HSD_Text pointer arrays at later offsets.
Added a TmData forward declaration to src/melee/gm/forward.h.
Changed gm_804771C4 in gm_17C0.c from static UNK_T gm_804771C4[349] to static TmData gm_804771C4.
Changed gm_8018F634 from returning UNK_T to returning TmData*, and used it as the Tournament data accessor.

Improved gm_17C0.h prototypes for functions near 8018F5F0-8019044C, including fn_8018F5F0 as char*, fn_8018F62C as s32 fn_8018F62C(HSD_GObj*), fn_8018FDC4 as void fn_8018FDC4(HSD_JObj*, f32, f32, f32), and fn_8019044C as void fn_8019044C(HSD_JObj*, f32).
Improved gmtou.h prototypes from broad UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS forms to concrete void returns and HSD_GObj*, void*, s32, u32, or UNK_T parameters where evidence allowed.
smart_moves: Used a small inline GetTmData wrapper around gm_8018F634, making repeated Tournament data accesses readable while preserving the recovered gm_8018F634 linkage.
Recovered gm_804771C4 as a TmData object instead of leaving it as an untyped UNK_T array, enabling field-based matches such as selection reads from x0 and bracket/animation offsets from x4 or pad_x8.
Separated Tournament-only static/local declarations into gmtou.static.h rather than placing all recovered structs in the public gmtou.h API.
Kept uncertain structure recovery honest with a TODO on TmAnimTimers noting that the layout is not exactly right.
Tightened many function prototypes at the same time as matching callsites, reducing reliance on UNK_RET, UNK_PARAMS, and UNK_T and making later decompilation easier.
Matched archive lifecycle pairs: on-enter loads multiple archives, and on-leave releases the corresponding HSD_Archive globals.
Used recovered baselib helpers such as GET_JOBJ, HSD_JObjGetChild, and HSD_JObjGetNext to express the JObj traversal directly instead of opaque pointer arithmetic.
decomp_lessons: When replacing a global untyped byte/word array with a real struct, update both the definition and the accessor return type so downstream code can use field accesses naturally.
For mode-specific globals that are shared with another C file, place the struct type in a shared types header or forward header, but avoid leaking static/data-definition headers into public headers.
Archive-heavy on-enter/on-leave functions can often be matched by identifying resource names and pairing lbArchive_80016DBC loads with lbArchive_80016EFC frees.
Animation callbacks often become simple once the GObj-to-JObj relationship is typed; GET_JOBJ plus small timer structs can replace broad UNK_PARAMS prototypes.
It is acceptable to add approximate structs with padding and a TODO when the offsets are necessary for a match but the semantic field names/layout are not fully known.
Converting prototypes away from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS is valuable even for functions not fully implemented in the same PR, because it improves type propagation for nearby matches.
Public headers should be self-contained, but .c files should include their own public .h first to prove that self-containment; private static headers should remain included only from .c files.
naming_conventions: Tournament mode data was named with the Tm prefix: TmData, TmBoxArrays, and TmAnimTimers.
Scene lifecycle functions retained the project convention of gm_<address>_OnEnter and gm_<address>_OnLeave.
Unidentified functions retained address-based fn_<address> names until stronger semantic names are known.
Partially understood struct fields used offset-style names such as x0, x4, xE, xF, x1C, pad_x8, x4E0, x4E4, x4E8, x500, and x518.
The helper accessor was named GetTmData, clearly exposing the intent of gm_8018F634 without renaming the external symbol.
The static/private header followed the module naming pattern gmtou.static.h.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Typed HSD_GObj* parameters enabled direct GET_JOBJ use in callbacks such as fn_8019BF18, fn_8019BF8C, fn_8019C6AC, fn_8019CBFC, and fn_8019CFA4.
Used explicit timer reset thresholds and increments to match animation-frame behavior, including limits such as >800, >0x77, >0x9F, >0x3C, and <0xA.
Preserved likely compiler behavior by keeping apparently unused TmData* locals from GetTmData in functions such as fn_8019C6AC and fn_8019CBFC.

Matched archive-loading calls by using string literals visible in the binary, including GmTou1p, TmBox.dat, tournament_box2_array, tournament_box3_array, tournament_box4_array, GmTou3p, GmTou4p, ScGamTour_scene_data, and SIS_TournamentData.
Used padding in TmData to place known HSD_Text pointer fields at observed offsets rather than over-specifying unknown middle data.
Changed fn_8018F62C to take HSD_GObj* while still using M2C_FIELD(arg0, s32*, 0x2C), showing a transitional match where the containing type is known but the field access is not yet fully modeled.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: Each .c file should include its own .h file at the top before any other includes, as a guarantee that the .h file works standalone without needing prior declarations.

review_feedback.author: r-burns
review_feedback.comment: .static.h headers should only be included from .c files because they contain static definitions; including them from headers causes every includer to gain those definitions and can break data/bss sections.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs modifying src/melee/gm/gmtou.static.h to see whether TmAnimTimers layout was corrected.
Search for later renames or field recovery for struct TmData fields x0, x4, pad_x8, x4E0, x4E4, x4E8, x500, and x518.
Check subsequent PRs touching gm_804771C4 or gm_8018F634 for whether static linkage/header placement was adjusted after review.

Look for later matches of remaining gmtou stubs such as fn_8019BA08, fn_8019C048, fn_8019C3EC, fn_8019C570, fn_8019C744, fn_8019CA38, fn_8019CC74, fn_8019CDBC, fn_8019D074, fn_8019D1BC, fn_8019DD60, gm_8019E634, fn_8019EE80, and fn_8019EF08.
Search repository review history for additional .static.h guidance and include-order conventions.
